description: Oracle test facts for Q_f invariants and their connection to 3D Navier-Stokes regularity. Tests knowledge of vortex stretching, stretch-resistant variants, and the BKM criterion.

facts.context: The Beale-Kato-Majda (1984) criterion states that a smooth 3D Euler/Navier-Stokes solution blows up at time T if and only if:
facts.truth: ∫₀ᵀ ||ω||_{L^∞} dt = ∞ (vorticity maximum becomes infinite in finite time)

facts.context: The 3D vorticity equation ∂ω/∂t + (u·∇)ω = (ω·∇)u + ν∇²ω contains the term (ω·∇)u which is called:
facts.truth: vortex stretching (unique to 3D, absent in 2D, can amplify |ω|)

facts.context: For 3D Euler vorticity, under vortex stretching by factor s (tube stretches, thins), the standard Q_f = ∫∫ ω(x)·ω(y) f(|x-y|) d³x d³y transforms as:
distractors: Q_f goes to zero under stretching

facts.context: In 3D Euler, the kinetic energy E = (1/2)∫|u|²dx corresponds to Q_{1/r}. Under vortex stretching, energy is:
facts.truth: exactly conserved (stretching redistributes but cannot create energy)

facts.context: The ratio R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} for 3D vortex dynamics under stretching is:
facts.truth: more stable than either Q_f alone (both scale as s², ratio partially cancels)

facts.context: Numerical tests of R_f = Q_{exp} / Q_{inv} on 3D vortex ring evolution show stretch resistance of:
facts.truth: 2% variation under stretching (vs 60% for standard Q_f)

facts.context: For 3D Euler, the enstrophy-normalized Q_f / Ω (where Ω = ∫|ω|² dx) behaves under stretching as:
facts.truth: 17% variation (better than 60% for unnormalized, worse than R_f at 2%)

facts.context: The curvature-weighted Q_f^κ = ∫∫ κ(x)κ(y) ω·ω f(r) d³x d³y shows stretch resistance of:
facts.truth: 4% variation (emphasizes bent regions, reduces when tubes straighten)

facts.context: For 2D Navier-Stokes with viscosity ν, the Q_f quantities decay as:
facts.truth: dQ_f/dt ∝ -ν (linear in viscosity), recovering Euler conservation as ν→0
distractors: Q_f is exact even with viscosity

facts.context: For a Gaussian vortex with width σ and circulation Γ, the relationship between Q_r and ||ω||_∞ is:
For 2D Euler/Navier-Stokes, Q_f conservation combined with BKM implies:
facts.truth: no finite-time blowup (consistent with known 2D global regularity)

facts.context: The main obstacle to using Q_f for 3D NS regularity is:
facts.truth: vortex stretching breaks Q_f conservation (except for energy Q_{1/r})

facts.context: Physically, the ratio R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} measures:
facts.truth: locality of vorticity interactions (how much dynamics depends on nearby vs distant vorticity)

facts.context: In 3D ideal fluids, the helicity H = ∫ u·ω dx is:
facts.truth: exactly conserved (topological invariant of vortex line linking)

facts.context: The Clay Millennium Problem for 3D Navier-Stokes asks whether:
facts.truth: smooth solutions remain smooth for all time, or if singularities can develop

facts.context: The combined score (stretch + evolution conservation) for R_f = Q_{exp}/Q_{inv} is:
facts.truth: 0.59% (best among all tested variants including enstrophy-norm and curvature-weighted)
